conf-proc-title-group.conf-normalized-title: Energy Efficiency and Agricultural Engineering (EE&AE), 2025 10th International Conference on

conf-meta.conf-name: 2025 10th International Conference on Energy Efficiency and Agricultural Engineering (EE&AE)

title-group.article-title: Investigation of Brake Deceleration of Electric Scooter

conf-article-meta.aff: Department of Transport Engineering and Technologies, Technical University of Varna Varna, Bulgaria \\ r_dimitrov@tu-varna.bg
Department of Transport Engineering and Technologies, Technical University of Varna Varna, Bulgaria \\ dan.ivanov@tu-varna.bg
Department of Transport Engineering and Technologies, Technical University of Varna Varna, Bulgaria \\ velichka.georgieva@tu-varna.bg
Department of Transport Engineering and Technologies, Technical University of Varna Varna, Bulgaria \\ plamen93n@gmail.com

Dep. of Automotive Engineering, Wrocław University of Science and Technology \\ Wroclaw

Personal use is permitted, but republication/redistribution requires IEEE permission. See https://www.ieee.org/publications/rights/index.html for more information.

abstract.p: Over the past few years, the use of electric scooters has increased significantly. According to the latest data, there are about $\mathbf{1 0 0, 0 0 0}$ electric scooters in Bulgaria. Accidents with this type of vehicle are constantly increasing, with serious accidents for the first half of 2025. are about 470, of which 8 are fatal [7]. This article presents a study showing the possibilities for effective braking of the described vehicles. The maximum braking deceleration, average braking deceleration, braking distance and other specific parameters in an emergency braking situation are examined.

sec.label: I.
sec.title: Introduction
sec.p: The speed of the vehicle is directly related to its braking time and, accordingly, to the braking deceleration. At higher speeds, the braking distance is longer. This is because the distance a vehicle has to travel to stop increases as its speed increases [1], [4]. The speed of the vehicle is determined according to the distance traveled, because the two values are directly related. Determining the speed according to the road traveled by the vehicle is the most common way to determine it in expert practice.

Braking lag is actually deceleration. It depends on several factors: the speed of the car, road conditions, weather conditions, grip and condition of the tires, the condition of the braking system, the behavior and reaction of the driver [3], [5].

Auto technical expertise is the process of examining and evaluating a vehicle, its components and systems in order to determine the cause of the accident. Braking deceleration is one of the key parameters that are taken into account in these evaluations. The correct selection of braking deceleration and its measurement is extremely important for the correct conclusions in expert analysis. There are several factors that affect the length of the braking distance and, accordingly, the braking deceleration:
Speed: The higher the speed of the vehicle, the longer the braking distance. This is because it takes more time and distance to slow down;
Road conditions: The presence of water, dust or other substances can significantly increase braking distance, as it reduces traction between the tires and the road;

The speed of electric scooters can vary from $25 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$ to over $55 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$. The electric scooter is characterized by an electric motor, battery, folding design, lights and brakes. The power of the electric motor affects acceleration and the ability to overcome slopes. The battery determines the range. LED headlights and brake lights increase safety. Disc and electric brakes provide effective braking. Electric scooters usually have disc brakes and electric brakes that provide reliable braking.
The brakes of electric scooters are of the following types:

1.Foot brake - it is activated by pressing with your foot and acts on the rear wheel (older or budget model). This system has a simple design, no control electronics, but allows less control over the scooter when braking, and stability is dependent on the change in the position of the body in a transverse direction.

2.Drum brakes - a variation of mechanical braking systems, but use a brake drum mounted on the wheel of the wheel. These systems have a simple design and long-term maintenance. Compared to disc brake systems, they have lower efficiency and are sensitive to moisture and water.
3.Mechanical disc brakes - brake discs, usually actuated by levers, mechanically. This braking system provides high-quality and predictable braking, good handling on dry and wet surfaces.

4.Hydraulic disc brakes - hydraulic brake systems are more effective by providing more braking force on the brake discs by applying less force on the driver's side. As a result, less lever travel is obtained, stable braking under different conditions.

5.Brake by energy regeneration (to motor braking) - in the process of braking, the electric motor is in generator mode, creates resistance to the drive wheel, creates energy through which it charges the battery. It is activated via the brake lever or through the regeneration mode. It helps to gradually reduce speed, but is not effective when driving at high speeds.

6.Combined braking systems - combine braking by energy regeneration with mechanical or hydraulic brakes for better control of the vehicle when braking and increase braking performance. These systems offer smooth braking, combined with less wear on the friction surfaces, as well as increased durability.

The purpose of this study is to investigate and determine in the conditions of emergency braking - the braking deceleration, braking distance and the parameters in the process of braking electric scooters at speeds of $20 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$ and $30 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$.

sec.label: A.
sec.title: Used Apparatus
sec.p: In the studies to determine the braking parameters, a device equipped with an accelerometer and the possibility of mounting the examined vehicles is used.

The meter used is shown in Figure 1, indicating its characteristics and functions.
Fig. 1.
Apparatus

The BRAKE TESTER is designed to measure brake deceleration for a very wide range of vehicles, including: cars, trucks, motorcycles, two- and four-wheeled vehicles, etc. The device allows you to record dynamic phenomena of the braking process. The parameters that are recorded are: maximum braking deceleration, average braking deceleration, speed at the start of braking, braking distance, etc. The device is suitable for measuring all kinds of brake systems, mechanical and hydraulic.

A built-in communication connection allows the results after the measurement to be transferred to a PC for subsequent processing. Thanks to the use of an innovative data processing algorithm, the device automatically eliminates the harmful effect of tilting the vehicle frame from the horizontal during braking, thanks to which the graphs of the distance traveled, speed and deceleration are automatically corrected through the use of special software.

Figures 2, 3 and 4 show the electric scooters used in the studies.
•
Electrical scooter „Harley Maxi“
The features of the scooter are:
Power of the electric motor - brushless, $3000 ~\mathrm{W}, 60 ~\mathrm{V}$;
Battery Durability - 12Ah lithium battery;
Tire size - 110/90-16 (front tire size) 215/40-12 (rear tire size)
The features of the scooter are:
Motor power 300W;
Battery capacity - 474Wh up to 45 km on a single charge;
Gyms with a size of $8.5^{\prime \prime}$ are made of special material, resistant to acceleration and adoption, have good durability and adaptability on the road;
Transmission - automatic gears and three modes of operation (ECO - mode recovering energy when braking, Ddirect or standard, S - sport;

Brakes - rear brakes with an AV regenerative antiblocking system (120 mm). The rear wheel uses a larger brake disc with more braking power, while the front wheel is equipped with an E-ABS regenerating anti-lock braking system. When braking, the front and rear braking systems are activated one after the other, shortening the braking distance, ensuring safe braking.
The efficiency of energy recovery depends on the operating mode of the scooter, as well as on the set by an application on the mobile phone connected to it.
The front brake is not always activated only when the Scooter is in ECO mode;
The features of the scooter are:

Power of the electric motor - brushless, $1000 ~\mathrm{W}, 52 ~\mathrm{V}$; Battery Durability - 20.8Ah lithium battery; Tire size - 10 -inch tubeless tires; Transmission - automatic gears 3 forward; Brakes - front drum brake, rear disc brake; Top speed $60 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$;

sec.label: III.
sec.title: Results
sec.p: The studies were carried out on a dry, horizontal road section, new asphalt, without bumps and undergrowth.
Options for braking with front brake, rear brake and both at the same time have been explored.

Figure 5 shows an example of a summarized experimental information characteristic, recorded by the device used.
Fig. 5.
Experimental data
The data are from a study of an electric scooter "Hiaomi Mi Electric Scooter M365 Pro", when braking by pressing the rear brake, when operating the scooter S (sport).
The experimental results obtained at a speed of $20 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$ are shown in Table 1. The data is systematized when the front brake, rear brake and both are applied at the same time.
The experimental results obtained at a speed of $30 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$ are shown in Table 2.
Table 3 presents the arithmetic mean values of the investigated parameters during the braking process.

The parameters presented in the tables are as follows:
•
$\mathrm{a}_{\text {max }}$ - maximum value of braking deceleration; •
$\mathrm{a}_{\text {average }}$ - average value of braking deceleration on braking process; •
$\mathrm{V}_{\text {stop }}$ - speed in beginning of brake; •
$\mathrm{L}_{\text {stop }}$ - stopping distance; •
$F_{p u s h}$ - force of pushing for brake system activation.
Table I. \caption{}
$\mathbf{2 0 k m} \boldmath{/} \mathbf{h}$
Harley Maxi Xiaomi Mi M365 Kukirin G2 Max

$\mathrm{L}_{\text {stop }}[\mathrm{m}]$ 2,91 5,48 4,19
$\mathrm{F}_{\text {push }}$ [N] 22,69 23,63 23,16

sec.label: IV.
sec.title: Conclusions
sec.p: For an electric scooter when braking from a speed of $20 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$ with front brake, the maximum braking deceleration is $4.38 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$, when braking with rear brake, the maximum braking deceleration is $4.86 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$, when braking with both brakes applied, the maximum braking deceleration is $6.25 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$.

When braking from a speed of $30 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$ with the front brake, the maximum braking deceleration is $4,29 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$, when braking with rear brake, the maximum braking deceleration is $5,57 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$, when braking with both brakes applied, the maximum braking deceleration is $6,93 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$.

The average maximum braking deceleration for an electric scooter when braking with front brake is $4,34 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$, when braking with rear braking, the maximum average braking deceleration is $5,21 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$, when braking with both brakes applied, the average maximum braking deceleration is $6,59 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$.

ack.title: Acknowledgment
ack.p: The article is funded under project PD14 from internal competitions for scientific projects funded by the state budget - 2025 of the Technical University of Varna.

ref.mixed-citation: Jeppsson H, Lubbe N. “Simulating Automated Emergency Braking with and without Torricelli Vacuum Emergency Braking for cyclists: Effect of brake deceleration and sensor field-of-view on accidents, injuries and fatalities,” Accident Analysis & Prevention, vol. 142, 105538, 2020.
Li S, Li P, Yao Y, Han X, Xu Y, Chen L. “Analysis of drivers' deceleration behavior based on naturalistic driving data,” Traffic injury prevention, vol.21, no. 1, 2020, pp. 42–7.

Bošković BM, Stojanović NR, Grujić IL, Babić SZ. “Experimental investigation of the influence of deceleration on brake elements temperature in order to improve traffic safety,” Thermal Science, vol. 28, no. 4A, 2024, pp. 2881–2889.
Deligianni SP, Quddus M, Morris A, Anvuur A, Reed S. “Analyzing and modeling drivers' deceleration behavior from normal driving,” Transportation research record, vol. 2663, no. 1, 2017, pp. 134–141.
Vangi D, Virga A. “Evaluation of emergency braking deceleration for accident reconstruction,” Vehicle System Dynamics, vol. 45, no. 10. 2007, pp. 895–910.
Eubanks JJ, Haight WR, Malmsbury RN, Casteel DA. “A Comparison of Devices Used to Measure Vehicle Braking Deceleration,” SAE Technical Paper; 1993.
